description: Entity relationship map for DetectHiddenFees.com knowledge graph. Defines how entities, concepts, and topics connect.

relationships.description: DetectHiddenFees platform detects hidden fees in consumer financial documents using AI analysis.
DetectHiddenFees provides AI-powered contract review as a core service.
DetectHiddenFees provides AI-powered financial document analysis as a core service.
DetectHiddenFees offers AI-powered financial advisory guidance to consumers.
DetectHiddenFees promotes consumer financial transparency as its core mission.
DetectHiddenFees performs contract risk analysis as part of its AI review capabilities.
DetectHiddenFees is powered by document intelligence technology for extracting and analyzing unstructured documents.
AI contract review identifies hidden fees, overcharges, and pricing manipulation in legal agreements.
AI contract review analyzes construction contracts for change order traps, material upcharges, and scope gaps.
AI contract review includes contract risk assessment for financial, legal, and operational risks.
AI contract review examines subscription and service agreements for automatic renewal and hidden fee clauses.
AI financial analysis identifies hidden fees, duplicate charges, and billing errors in financial documents.
AI financial analysis examines invoices for overcharges, duplicate billing, and incorrect line items.
AI financial analysis reviews subscription bills for hidden recurring charges and fee increases.
AI financial analysis works across bills, invoices, bank statements, receipts, and estimates.
AI financial advisor promotes consumer financial transparency through data-driven guidance.
AI financial advisor uses AI financial analysis results to provide personalized consumer guidance.
AI financial advisor uses AI contract review findings to provide negotiation recommendations.
Consumer financial transparency combats hidden fees by demanding clear disclosure of all costs.
Consumer financial transparency enables pricing transparency by requiring clear, upfront cost information.
Hidden fees are commonly found during invoice analysis, including duplicate charges and inflated line items.
Hidden fees frequently appear in subscription services through automatic renewals and creeping price increases.
Hidden fees are prevalent in construction contracts via change orders, material markups, and vague scope language.
Hidden fees in banking include overdraft charges, maintenance fees, and ATM surcharges.
Hidden fees in medical billing include duplicate charges, facility fees, and upcoding practices.
Hidden fees in auto financing include dealer markup, unnecessary add-ons, and inflated interest rates.
Contract risk analysis identifies hidden fees as a primary category of financial risk in agreements.
Document intelligence technology powers AI contract review through OCR, entity extraction, and pattern matching.
Document intelligence technology powers AI financial analysis through data extraction and anomaly detection.
Invoice analysis detects hidden fees through line-item review and market rate comparison.
Pricing transparency prevents hidden fees by requiring clear disclosure of all costs upfront.
Pricing transparency supports consumer financial transparency by making costs visible and comparable.
Construction contracts are analyzed by specialized AI construction contract review for industry-specific hidden fees.
AI construction contract review specializes in construction-specific agreements, change orders, and contractor proposals.
AI agreement analyzer is related to AI contract review but covers a broader range of agreement types including leases, employment, and service agreements.
AI bill analysis is part of the broader AI financial analysis capability, specializing in bill-specific review.
AI bill analysis and invoice analysis are closely related, with bills and invoices sharing similar fee patterns.

relationshipTypes.primary_function: The main purpose or function connecting entities
relationshipTypes.service: A service provided by one entity to users
relationshipTypes.capability: What an entity is capable of doing
relationshipTypes.technology: Technology that powers or enables an entity
relationshipTypes.application: A specific use case or application domain
relationshipTypes.function: A specific function within a broader capability
relationshipTypes.dependency: One entity depends on or uses another
relationshipTypes.principle: A guiding principle or value connection
relationshipTypes.context: The context or domain where something occurs
relationshipTypes.sibling: Sibling entities at the same level
relationshipTypes.specialization: A specialized version of a broader entity
relationshipTypes.prevents: One entity prevents or mitigates another
